verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted protein that lacks an intrinsic transmembrane domain or GPI anchor but becomes stably surface-associated through binding to sperm plasma membrane proteins (PMCA4b and others) during epididymal maturation. A strongly bound population behaves as an integral membrane protein. The protein reaches the outer leaflet via its own mechanism—stable, wash-resistant anchoring to TM partner proteins—not transient recruitment.
